page: Amazon Web Services g5g.8xlarge is a graphics-intensive server powered by custom AWS Graviton processors. It features 32 dedicated physical cores on the ARM64 architecture using the AWS Neoverse-N1 model, operating at a clock speed of 2.5 GHz. The CPU configuration includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. This instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core. For graphics and acceleration workloads, it bundles a single NVIDIA T4G GPU from the Turing family, which includes 16 GB of dedicated VRAM. Local storage is not included on this instance type, and network performance is rated at a baseline bandwidth of 12 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate average performance across most multi-core and specialized workloads, with some single-core limitations. The stress-ng multi-core CPU test scored 48574.86, and the Geekbench multi-core score reached 11551.0, both placing in the average tier. Single-core performance is weaker, with a Geekbench single-core score of 952.0 and a weak rating for single-threaded text processing and compression tasks. Memory bandwidth is robust for small cached blocks at 1892.97 GB/sec, but drops to 161.7 GB/sec for large uncached blocks. In LLM inference tests, the server achieved an average prompt processing speed of 31.98 tokens/sec and text generation speed of 15.24 tokens/sec using a medium 7B model, though text generation on smaller 135M models registered as poor.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the g5g.8xlarge offers a specialized resource profile by pairing energy-efficient ARM64 architecture with dedicated NVIDIA GPU acceleration. This combination provides a cost-effective alternative to x86-based GPU instances for workloads optimized for ARM. The server is suited for graphics-intensive applications, ray tracing, software compilation, and medium-scale machine learning inference tasks that can leverage the NVIDIA T4G GPU and the 32 dedicated Graviton cores.
description: Amazon Web Services g5g.8xlarge is an ARM64-based server designed for graphics-intensive and accelerated workloads. Powered by an AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor, it features 32 dedicated physical cores running at 2.5 GHz, paired with 64.0 GB of system memory. The hardware configuration includes a single NVIDIA T4G GPU with 16 GB of VRAM, while local storage is absent. Network performance is supported by a 12 Gbps baseline bandwidth. Benchmark data shows average multi-core CPU performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 48574.86 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 11551.0, though single-core performance and memory scores are relatively weak. The instance delivers average performance for medium-scale LLM inference and ray tracing. This server is suited for ARM-optimized graphics applications, software compilation, and machine learning inference workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services g5g.8xlarge is an ARM64 server featuring 32 dedicated Graviton cores, 64 GB RAM, and an NVIDIA T4G GPU with 16 GB VRAM, designed for graphics-intensive and accelerated workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services g5g.8xlarge features 32 ARM64 cores, 64 GB RAM, and an NVIDIA T4G GPU for accelerated graphics and machine learning workloads.
tagline: An ARM64-powered accelerated instance combining dedicated physical cores with NVIDIA GPU capabilities for graphics and machine learning workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated ARM64 physical cores powered by AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processors
Integrated NVIDIA T4G GPU with 16 GB of dedicated VRAM
64.0 GB of system memory and 12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Average performance in multi-core benchmarks, ray tracing, and medium LLM inference
Suited for ARM-optimized graphics rendering, software compilation, and machine learning
